summary: Excellent matrix capture of a historic show, highlighted by an extraordinary Ornette Coleman/Space segment and stellar Broken Arrow debut.
review: Both reviewers praise the matrix source for its deep bass response and immersive room feel, calling it a perfect blend of soundboard clarity and audience ambience. The performance shines on the second set, with the Ornette Coleman‑inspired space jam, a groundbreaking Broken Arrow debut, and an inspired Johnny B. Goode that rank among the band’s best late‑career moments.

ai_rating.rationale: High praise for both musical performance (standout second set, experimental space) and recording quality (excellent matrix), offset by only two reviews which limits confidence to medium.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers note deep bass capture, crisp soundboard detail combined with vivid audience ambience; no reported drop‑outs or muddiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as less compelling, while the second set delivers experimental brilliance with Ornette Coleman‑inspired space and powerful late‑show jams.
